title: scolymus hispanicus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Scolymus hispanicus subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Western artichoke thistle, is a plant species within the sunflower family, characterized by its thistle-like flowers and edible buds. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The Western artichoke thistle is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant's medicinal properties can be harnessed through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
